title: Anti-bit-rot refresh process + drift detection
context: Tutorials that pin a tool version become broken when the tool releases a new version — this is among the highest-likelihood risks listed in PLAN.md. Without an explicit, documented refresh cadence and a CI mechanism to detect drift, broken tutorials silently mislead users and erode trust in the project's reproducibility claims. Depends on harness-014 (which provides the CI harness that detects breakage) and the body of delivered M1/M2 tutorials.
objective: Document and implement an anti-bit-rot refresh process: a cadenced procedure for re-running delivered tutorials against new tool releases, a CI mechanism that flags breakage, and a rule that breakages become explicit maintenance tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented refresh cadence specifies how often delivered tutorials are re-run against new upstream tool releases (e.g., monthly or on upstream release).
The CI harness (harness-014) is configured to flag breakage when a delivered tutorial fails against the latest tool release.
A rule is documented and committed: each breakage detected becomes an explicit maintenance task in the project backlog with its own ID.
Lockfile update procedure is documented: lockfiles are updated deliberately with a record of what changed and why, not automatically.
A Steward or Maintainer is identified as the owner of the refresh cadence.
The process is committed as a document and is reviewed and confirmed by the Maintainer.
output: A committed anti-bit-rot refresh process document and CI configuration that detects tutorial drift, with a documented cadence and breakage-to-task escalation rule, ensuring delivered tutorials remain runnable as tools release new versions.
